goal: Tighten seal risk guidance so Risk-heavy Hub stays signal-rich

summary.title: Tighten seal risk guidance for signal-rich Risk-heavy views
summary.what: Updated the seal --prepare instruction so agents only put concrete hazards into summary.risks. The guidance now explicitly allows risks for concrete failure modes, compatibility breaks, data loss or corruption, security or privacy issues, performance or scale regressions, user-visible misbehavior, and maintenance hazards. It explicitly excludes verification notes, tests-not-run notes, review guidance, cosmetic concerns, generic unknown-risk disclaimers, implementation summaries, scope limitations, and ordinary follow-up work. Added a focused test that locks the instruction wording so future edits do not silently loosen the contract.
summary.why: Hub's Risk-heavy page is only useful if summary.risks remains a high-signal audit index. Existing sealed intents showed that agents were using risks too broadly for verification notes, follow-ups, limitations, and review context. Tightening the seal instruction addresses the source of future noise without changing historical data or expanding the SealResult schema.
summary.user_goal: 收紧 risks 的写作标准，让 Risk-heavy intents 更像真正的风险索引，而不是几乎所有 intent 都会出现的噪音页。

decisions.chose: Tighten the seal --prepare instruction instead of filtering risks in Hub.
decisions.rationale: Hub should render the sealed record, not guess which historical risk strings are valid. The right place to improve future data quality is the authoring prompt that produces SealResult.

decisions.chose: Keep the existing SealResult schema and add stricter field guidance.
decisions.rationale: Adding limitations/review_notes/verification fields would require broader schema and UI migration. The immediate problem is overfilling risks, so a prompt-level contract is the smallest effective fix.

decisions.chose: Add a focused unit test that asserts the instruction includes the concrete allowed risk classes and excluded non-risk classes.
decisions.rationale: The guidance is product behavior for agents; testing the wording prevents accidental removal when seal instructions are edited later.

rejected.reason: Historical sealed intents are an audit record. Rewriting them would be heavier and semantically questionable; this change improves future seals.

rejected.alternative: Make Hub classify and hide weak risks heuristically.
rejected.reason: String heuristics would be brittle and would make Hub a second source of truth. It should show what was sealed.

rejected.alternative: Extend SealResult with limitations, verification, or review_notes now.
rejected.reason: That may be useful later, but it is a larger contract and renderer change than needed to stop agents from putting non-risks into risks.

risks: Instruction-only enforcement depends on agents following seal --prepare guidance; callers that construct SealResult directly can still overfill summary.risks.
No historical cleanup is performed, so current Hub Risk-heavy output remains noisy until enough future intents are sealed under the stricter guidance.
followups: If agents keep overfilling risks, consider adding schema-level fields for limitations, verification, and review_notes so non-risk context has an explicit destination.
Consider a future audit/backfill tool that classifies old risks without mutating sealed intent history.

architectural_claims: Risk quality is enforced at SealResult authoring time via seal --prepare guidance rather than by Hub render-time filtering.
SealResult schema remains unchanged; the change is additive instruction text plus tests.
Hub Risk-heavy remains a faithful view of sealed summary.risks.
behavioral_changes: mainline seal --prepare now tells agents to keep summary.risks limited to concrete hazards.

mainline seal --prepare now tells agents to exclude verification notes, tests-not-run notes, review guidance, cosmetic concerns, generic disclaimers, implementation summaries, scope limitations, and ordinary follow-up work from summary.risks.
